trace_policy: Every trace row links a draft support claim to a support score, CMT mapping, MIPVU unit list, sentence ID, document metadata, source text path, and historical corroboration where available.

traces.claim_text: Lyceum Address contributes moderate support evidence for sacred object (score 2.0/4): The address figures the nation as a living collective body but does not yet supply battlefield sacrifice or strong enemy-as-bringer-of-death evidence.
traces.claim_boundary: Draft evidentiary-support assessment from reviewed pilot evidence; not proof, diagnosis, or monocausal historical explanation.
traces.weight_rationale: Standard early public-address benchmark; important for diachronic setup but not war enactment.
traces.evidence_span: In the great journal of things happening under the sun, we, the American people, find our account running under date of the nineteenth century of the Christian era.
traces.rival_reading: Events under the sun are not literally a ledger, but the sentence frames history through accounting.
traces.sentence_text: In the great journal of things happening under the sun, we, the American people, find our account running under date of the nineteenth century of the Christian era.
traces.document_title: Address Before the Young Men's Lyceum of Springfield, Illinois
traces.source_citation: Project Gutenberg EBook #3253 — The Papers and Writings of Abraham Lincoln, Complete, ed. Arthur Brooks Lapsley. Date note: Lapsley edition reads '1837'; correct date is 1838-01-27 per Basler, Collected Works vol. 1, p. 108.

historical_corroboration.topic: Lyceum republican survival rhetoric before the Civil War
historical_corroboration.summary: The Lyceum Address supplies early body-politic survival language, but its alignment is primarily rhetorical and civic rather than direct war enactment.
historical_corroboration.claim_boundary: Useful for diachronic setup; does not by itself establish historical enactment.

review_limitations: Trace rows are draft review aids, not final publication claims.
Publication-grade historical citations remain required where historical notes say so.
Scores are provisional and based on reviewed Lincoln pilot evidence.
Full-corpus MIPVU review and reliability adjudication are pending.
Historical alignment notes require publication-grade citations.

traces.claim_text: Gettysburg Address contributes strong support evidence for sacred object (score 3.0/4): Gettysburg strongly links battlefield death, national survival, civic dedication, and democratic renewal, while the enemy remains mostly unnamed.
traces.claim_boundary: Draft evidentiary-support assessment from reviewed pilot evidence; not proof, diagnosis, or monocausal historical explanation.
traces.evidence_span: We, when mounting the stage of existence, found ourselves the legal inheritors of these fundamental blessings.
traces.rival_reading: Collective historical existence is explicitly framed as appearing on a stage.
traces.sentence_text: We, when mounting the stage of existence, found ourselves the legal inheritors of these fundamental blessings.
traces.document_title: Address Delivered at the Dedication of the Cemetery at Gettysburg
traces.source_citation: Project Gutenberg EBook #4 — Bliss copy (Lincoln's final corrected manuscript, the only one he signed)

historical_corroboration.topic: Gettysburg battlefield death, cemetery dedication, and public mourning
historical_corroboration.summary: Gettysburg links actual battlefield death, cemetery dedication, and public mourning to Lincoln's symbolic conversion of death into national obligation.
historical_corroboration.claim_boundary: Supports historical alignment for public mourning and battlefield death, not a claim that the address caused the deaths.

review_limitations: Trace rows are draft review aids, not final publication claims.

traces.claim_text: Gettysburg Address contributes strong support evidence for sacred object (score 3.0/4): Gettysburg strongly links battlefield death, national survival, civic dedication, and democratic renewal, while the enemy remains mostly unnamed.
traces.claim_boundary: Draft evidentiary-support assessment from reviewed pilot evidence; not proof, diagnosis, or monocausal historical explanation.
traces.expression: uprear / edifice / transmit / unprofaned / undecayed / untorn
traces.evidence_span: Theirs was the task (and nobly they performed it) to possess themselves, and through themselves us, of this goodly land, and to uprear upon its hills and its valleys a political edifice of liberty and equal rights; it is ours only to transmit these--the former unprofaned by the foot of an invader, the latter undecayed by the lapse of time and untorn by usurpation--to the latest generation that fate shall permit the world to know.
traces.rival_reading: Political institutions are explicitly construed as a built edifice.
traces.sentence_text: Theirs was the task (and nobly they performed it) to possess themselves, and through themselves us, of this goodly land, and to uprear upon its hills and its valleys a political edifice of liberty and equal rights; it is ours only to transmit these--the former unprofaned by the foot of an invader, the latter undecayed by the lapse of time and untorn by usurpation--to the latest generation that fate shall permit the world to know.

traces.expression: uprear / edifice / transmit / unprofaned / undecayed / untorn
traces.evidence_span: Theirs was the task (and nobly they performed it) to possess themselves, and through themselves us, of this goodly land, and to uprear upon its hills and its valleys a political edifice of liberty and equal rights; it is ours only to transmit these--the former unprofaned by the foot of an invader, the latter undecayed by the lapse of time and untorn by usurpation--to the latest generation that fate shall permit the world to know.
traces.rival_reading: Political institutions are explicitly construed as a built edifice.
traces.sentence_text: Theirs was the task (and nobly they performed it) to possess themselves, and through themselves us, of this goodly land, and to uprear upon its hills and its valleys a political edifice of liberty and equal rights; it is ours only to transmit these--the former unprofaned by the foot of an invader, the latter undecayed by the lapse of time and untorn by usurpation--to the latest generation that fate shall permit the world to know.
